title: Add password protection to the admin panel

requirements.summary: Add password gate to admin panel with session persistence
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to the admin panel route shows a password entry screen before any admin content is visible.
Entering the correct password 'Mrudani.jade.jyo.hem' and submitting grants access to the full admin panel.
Entering an incorrect password displays a visible error message and does not grant access.
The password entry screen uses the Jade luxury dark aesthetic (charcoal background, gold accents, cream text).
Once authenticated within a session, the user is not re-prompted on page reload (sessionStorage persistence).
The password input masks the entered characters (type='password').

file_operations.description: Create the admin panel page component with password gate logic. Before rendering any admin content, check sessionStorage for 'adminAuthenticated' flag. If not authenticated, display a centered password entry screen with a password input field (type='password'), an 'Enter' button, and error message state. On submit, compare the entered password to the hardcoded value 'Mrudani.jade.jyo.hem'. If correct, set sessionStorage 'adminAuthenticated' to 'true' and update component state to render the admin panel content (product management interface with add/edit capabilities). If incorrect, display an error message 'Incorrect password. Please try again.' The password entry screen should use the Jade luxury dark aesthetic: charcoal background (bg-charcoal), gold accents for the button and focus states (text-gold, border-gold), and cream text (text-cream). The admin panel content should include a product management interface with forms for adding new products (name, price, image URL, category selection) and functionality to interact with the backend addProduct method.

Add a new route definition for the admin panel page at path '/admin'. Import the AdminPage component and register it as a child route in the TanStack Router configuration alongside the existing routes (landing, catalog, order-tracking, order-confirmation, profile). Ensure the admin route is wrapped by the root layout route so it has access to CartProvider and the Layout component.

Add a navigation link to the admin panel in the site header navigation. Include a new Link component pointing to '/admin' with the text 'Admin' styled consistently with existing navigation links. Position it appropriately in the navigation menu (e.g., after the Profile link or in a location that makes sense for administrative access). Use existing Tailwind classes for hover and active states matching the luxury brand aesthetic.
